page: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC processor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate a strong performance profile, particularly in memory operations and single-core tasks. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier, while the multi-core performance falls in the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show strong results for both large-block read and write operations, and the PassMark memory score is in the top tier. For text generation workloads using small and medium language models, the server achieves top-tier and strong performance, respectively.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for compute-optimized tasks rather than memory-intensive databases. It is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, medium-scale LLM inference, and general-purpose compute tasks that benefit from fast local NVMe storage and strong single-core performance.
description: The Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared vCPUs (8 physical AMD EPYC cores) running at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance offers a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, aligning it with compute-optimized workloads. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth and efficient text-generation speeds for small and medium language models. However, its shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, dedicated processing power. This configuration is best suited for static web serving, software compilation, lightweight LLM inference, and general-purpose compute tasks that leverage fast local storage and high single-threaded efficiency.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors, fast NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance for scalable workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs (8 physical cores) running at 2.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores
Strong memory bandwidth and efficient small-to-medium LLM text generation
Suitable for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose compute
